spans.content: This document presents the technical architecture of a Multimodal GraphRAG System designed for
intelligent document parsing and knowledge graph construction. The system integrates MinerU for
document parsing, LangExtract for structured entity extraction, and a graph database for
The pipeline supports multiple document formats including PDF, DOCX, PPTX, and image files.
Extracted entities and relations are stored as graph nodes and edges, enabling semantic search
and question answering over large document collections.
MinerU Cloud API (v4) serves as the document parsing backend. It accepts PDF, DOCX, PPTX, PNG,
JPG, and HTML files. Output includes Markdown text, structured content_list.json, and extracted
LangExtract (v1.1.1) performs structured information extraction from plain text using few-shot
prompting with LLM backends (Gemini, OpenAI, or local Ollama). Each extraction includes
Extracted entities and relationships are stored in a graph database. Node types include: Person,
Organization, Location, Event, Concept. Edge types include: RELATED_TO, BELONGS_TO, CAUSED_BY,
The retrieval layer supports hybrid search combining vector similarity and graph traversal.
Query results are ranked by relevance score and returned with source document references.
This document presents the technical architecture of a Multimodal GraphRAG System designed for
intelligent document parsing and knowledge graph construction. The system integrates MinerU for
document parsing, LangExtract for structured entity extraction, and a graph database for
The pipeline supports multiple document formats including PDF, DOCX, PPTX, and image files.
Extracted entities and relations are stored as graph nodes and edges, enabling semantic search
and question answering over large document collections.
MinerU Cloud API (v4) serves as the document parsing backend. It accepts PDF, DOCX, PPTX, PNG,
JPG, and HTML files. Output includes Markdown text, structured content_list.json, and extracted
LangExtract (v1.1.1) performs structured information extraction from plain text using few-shot
prompting with LLM backends (Gemini, OpenAI, or local Ollama). Each extraction includes
Extracted entities and relationships are stored in a graph database. Node types include: Person,
Organization, Location, Event, Concept. Edge types include: RELATED_TO, BELONGS_TO, CAUSED_BY,
The retrieval layer supports hybrid search combining vector similarity and graph traversal.
Query results are ranked by relevance score and returned with source document references.
The end-to-end data pipeline consists of the following stages:
- Accept raw documents (PDF, DOCX, images, HTML)
- Submit to MinerU API for parsing
- Separate text blocks, tables, images, equations
- Map extractions to graph nodes and edges
- Build vector embeddings for semantic search

PDF (text). pdfNopipeline / vlm
PDF (scan). pdfYesvIlm
Word. docxNopipeline
PowerPoint.pptxNopipeline
Image.png / .jpgAutovIlm
HTML.htmlNoMinerU-HTML
